name: pde
transitions: real->protected (CR0.PE 0->1, CS far-jump to 32-bit flat code segment 0x08)
IDT load (lidt base=0x1000) + 1 #DE gate (vector 0, 32-bit interrupt gate)
#DE divide-by-zero: DIV by ECX=0 -> #DE (vector 0, no error code) -> de_handler -> resume past the DIV
#DE quotient overflow: EDX:EAX=0x1_0000_0000 / 1 -> quotient 0x100000000 (>32b) -> #DE -> de_handler -> resume
non-faulting DIV (255/5) commits normally; deterministic result (edx=(#DE<<8)|quot=0x233), isa-debug-exit 133
description: Bare-metal #DE (divide-error) IDT-delivery test for the review-response divide-family closure. DIV/IDIV now raise #DE (vector 0) on divide-by-zero and on quotient overflow, delivered through the verified S_INT_GATE FSM (FAULT: faulting EIP pushed). Per-record differential vs qemu-system-i386 (#DE is synchronous, so the gdbstub single-step golden delivers it). Boots real->protected (no paging), one vector-0 interrupt gate, two #DE triggers (div-by-zero + quotient overflow), de_handler counts + resumes past each divide, then a non-faulting DIV commits, deterministic result + isa-debug-exit.
